operation: First, a circle with 100 unit radius is sketched on the XY workplane, then extruded 130 units along the Z axis to create a solid base cylinder. Next, a workplane is established at the center of mass of the cylinder's top Z-facing face, which is transformed by rotating 90 degrees around the Z axis with a 50 unit negative Z offset, then rotated 90 degrees around the X axis with a 40 unit X offset and 15 unit Y offset. A 20 unit radius circle is sketched on this transformed workplane, and a full-depth blind cut is performed to create an angled through hole. Finally, the solid is split along a plane 50 units below the original top face of the cylinder, retaining only the bottom segment of the split part.
geometry: The model is a short right circular cylinder with a flat horizontal base and flat horizontal top face. A straight cylindrical through hole passes through the body at an oblique angle, entering through the curved lateral side wall of the cylinder and exiting through the upper flat face, oriented diagonally across the upper portion of the cylindrical structure.
function: This is a custom structural mechanical component intended for use as a fixture, spacer, or mounting insert, with an angled through hole designed to accommodate fasteners, alignment pins, or cabling routed at a non-orthogonal angle relative to the component's base surface.
